lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਖਹਿ ਕੀਤਾ ਆਪਣਾ ਧਰਿ ਕਚੀ ਪਕੀ ਸਾਰੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: dekheh keetaa aapanaa dhar kachee pakee saareeai |
lines.english: You behold Your creation, like the losing and winning dice of the earth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਕਰਣਾ ਕੀਓ ਕਲ ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਤੈ ਧਾਰੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: aape hee karanaa keeo kal aape hee tai dhaareeai |
lines.english: You Yourself created the creation; You Yourself infused Your power into it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਕੇ ਜੀਅ ਪਰਾਣ ਹਹਿ ਕਿਉ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਮਨਹੁ ਵਿਸਾਰੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jis ke jeea paraan heh kiau saahib manahu visaareeai |
lines.english: He who owns our soul, and our very breath of life - why should we forget that Lord and Master from our minds?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਆਇਆ ਸੋ ਚਲਸੀ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਈ ਆਈ ਵਾਰੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jo aaeaa so chalasee sabh koee aaee vaareeai |
lines.english: Whoever has come, shall depart; all shall have their turn.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹ ਕਿਨੇਹੀ ਆਸਕੀ ਦੂਜੈ ਲਗੈ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: eeh kinehee aasakee doojai lagai jaae |
lines.english: What sort of love is this, which clings to duality?

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣ ਹਥੀ ਆਪਣਾ ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਕਾਜੁ ਸਵਾਰੀਐ ॥੨੦॥
lines.translit: aapan hathee aapanaa aape hee kaaj savaareeai |20|
lines.english: With our own hands, let us resolve our own affairs. ||20||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਹਲਾ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੰਗੈ ਚੰਗਾ ਕਰਿ ਮੰਨੇ ਮੰਦੈ ਮੰਦਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: changai changaa kar mane mandai mandaa hoe |
lines.english: But one who feels good only when good is done for him, and feels bad when things go badly

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਆਸਕੁ ਕਾਂਢੀਐ ਸਦ ਹੀ ਰਹੈ ਸਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak aasak kaandteeai sad hee rahai samaae |
lines.english: O Nanak, he alone is called a lover, who remains forever immersed in absorption.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਕੁ ਏਹੁ ਨ ਆਖੀਐ ਜਿ ਲੇਖੈ ਵਰਤੈ ਸੋਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aasak ehu na aakheeai ji lekhai varatai soe |1|
lines.english: - do not call him a lover. He trades only for his own account. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲਾਮੁ ਜਬਾਬੁ ਦੋਵੈ ਕਰੇ ਮੁੰਢਹੁ ਘੁਥਾ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: salaam jabaab dovai kare mundtahu ghuthaa jaae |
lines.english: One who offers both respectful greetings and rude refusal to his master, has gone wrong from the very beginning.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਸੇਵਿਐ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਸੋ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸਦਾ ਸਮੑਾਲੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jit seviai sukh paaeeai so saahib sadaa samaaleeai |
lines.english: Serving Him, peace is obtained; meditate and dwell upon that Lord and Master forever.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਦੋਵੈ ਕੂੜੀਆ ਥਾਇ ਨ ਕਾਈ ਪਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak dovai koorreea thaae na kaaee paae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, both of his actions are false; he obtains no place in the Court of the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਕੀਤਾ ਪਾਈਐ ਆਪਣਾ ਸਾ ਘਾਲ ਬੁਰੀ ਕਿਉ ਘਾਲੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jit keetaa paaeeai aapanaa saa ghaal buree kiau ghaaleeai |
lines.english: Why do you do such evil deeds, that you shall have to suffer so?

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਦਾ ਮੂਲਿ ਨ ਕੀਚਈ ਦੇ ਲੰਮੀ ਨਦਰਿ ਨਿਹਾਲੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: mandaa mool na keechee de lamee nadar nihaaleeai |
lines.english: Do not do any evil at all; look ahead to the future with foresight.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਛੁ ਲਾਹੇ ਉਪਰਿ ਘਾਲੀਐ ॥੨੧॥
lines.translit: kichh laahe upar ghaaleeai |21|
lines.english: Do those deeds which shall bring you profit. ||21||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਨਾਲਿ ਨ ਹਾਰੀਐ ਤੇਵੇਹਾ ਪਾਸਾ ਢਾਲੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jiau saahib naal na haareeai tevehaa paasaa dtaaleeai |
lines.english: So throw the dice in such a way, that you shall not lose with your Lord and Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਲਾ ਕਰੇ ਘਣੇਰੀਆ ਖਸਮ ਨ ਪਾਏ ਸਾਦੁ ॥
lines.translit: galaa kare ghanereea khasam na paae saad |
lines.english: he may talk as much as he wants, but he shall not be pleasing to his Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਹਲਾ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਕਰੁ ਲਗੈ ਚਾਕਰੀ ਨਾਲੇ ਗਾਰਬੁ ਵਾਦੁ ॥
lines.translit: chaakar lagai chaakaree naale gaarab vaad |
lines.english: If a servant performs service, while being vain and argumentative,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਲਗਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਲਗਾ ਸੋ ਪਰਵਾਨੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak jis no lagaa tis milai lagaa so paravaan |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, if he merges with the one with whom he is attached, his attachment becomes acceptable. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰੇ ਤਾ ਕਿਛੁ ਪਾਏ ਮਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: aap gavaae sevaa kare taa kichh paae maan |
lines.english: But if he eliminates his self-conceit and then performs service, he shall be honored.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜੀਇ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਉਗਵੈ ਮੁਹ ਕਾ ਕਹਿਆ ਵਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jo jee hoe su ugavai muh kaa kahiaa vaau |
lines.english: Whatever is in the mind, comes forth; spoken words by themselves are just wind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੀਜੇ ਬਿਖੁ ਮੰਗੈ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਵੇਖਹੁ ਏਹੁ ਨਿਆਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: beeje bikh mangai amrit vekhahu ehu niaau |2|
lines.english: He sows seeds of poison, and demands Ambrosial Nectar. Behold - what justice is this? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਹਾ ਜਾਣੈ ਤੇਹੋ ਵਰਤੈ ਵੇਖਹੁ ਕੋ ਨਿਰਜਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: jehaa jaanai teho varatai vekhahu ko nirajaas |
lines.english: As he knows, he acts; behold, and see that it is so.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਲਿ ਇਆਣੇ ਦੋਸਤੀ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਰਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: naal eaane dosatee kade na aavai raas |
lines.english: Friendship with a fool never works out right.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬ ਸੇਤੀ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਨ ਚਲੈ ਕਹੀ ਬਣੈ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: saahib setee hukam na chalai kahee banai aradaas |
lines.english: No one can issue commands to the Lord Master; offer instead humble prayers.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਸਤੂ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਵਸਤੁ ਸਮਾਵੈ ਦੂਜੀ ਹੋਵੈ ਪਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: vasatoo andar vasat samaavai doojee hovai paas |
lines.english: One thing can be absorbed into another thing, but duality keeps them apart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂੜਿ ਕਮਾਣੈ ਕੂੜੋ ਹੋਵੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਿਫਤਿ ਵਿਗਾਸਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: koorr kamaanai koorro hovai naanak sifat vigaas |3|
lines.english: Practicing falsehood, only falsehood is obtained. O Nanak, through the Lord's Praise, one blossoms forth. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਲਿ ਇਆਣੇ ਦੋਸਤੀ ਵਡਾਰੂ ਸਿਉ ਨੇਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: naal eaane dosatee vaddaaroo siau nehu |
lines.english: Friendship with a fool, and love with a pompous person,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਇ ਇਆਣਾ ਕਰੇ ਕੰਮੁ ਆਣਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਰਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: hoe eaanaa kare kam aan na sakai raas |
lines.english: If a fool does a job, he cannot do it right.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਣੀ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਲੀਕ ਜਿਉ ਤਿਸ ਦਾ ਥਾਉ ਨ ਥੇਹੁ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: paanee andar leek jiau tis daa thaau na thehu |4|
lines.english: are like lines drawn in water, leaving no trace or mark. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਕਰੁ ਲਗੈ ਚਾਕਰੀ ਜੇ ਚਲੈ ਖਸਮੈ ਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: chaakar lagai chaakaree je chalai khasamai bhaae |
lines.english: If a servant, performing service, obeys the Will of his Master,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਇਕ ਅਧ ਚੰਗੀ ਕਰੇ ਦੂਜੀ ਭੀ ਵੇਰਾਸਿ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: je ik adh changee kare doojee bhee veraas |5|
lines.english: Even if he does something right, he does the next thing wrong. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੈ ਕਰੇ ਬਰਾਬਰੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਗੈਰਤਿ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: khasamai kare baraabaree fir gairat andar paae |
lines.english: But if he claims to be equal to his Master, he earns his Master's displeasure.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਰਮਤਿ ਤਿਸ ਨੋ ਅਗਲੀ ਓਹੁ ਵਜਹੁ ਭਿ ਦੂਣਾ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: huramat tis no agalee ohu vajahu bhi doonaa khaae |
lines.english: his honor increases, and he receives double his wages.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਜਹੁ ਗਵਾਏ ਅਗਲਾ ਮੁਹੇ ਮੁਹਿ ਪਾਣਾ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: vajahu gavaae agalaa muhe muhi paanaa khaae |
lines.english: He loses his entire salary, and is also beaten on his face with shoes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਦਾ ਦਿਤਾ ਖਾਵਣਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਕਹੀਐ ਸਾਬਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: jis daa ditaa khaavanaa tis kaheeai saabaas |
lines.english: Let us all celebrate Him, from whom we receive our nourishment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹ ਕਿਨੇਹੀ ਦਾਤਿ ਆਪਸ ਤੇ ਜੋ ਪਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: eeh kinehee daat aapas te jo paaeeai |
lines.english: What sort of gift is this, which we receive only by our own asking?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਨ ਚਲਈ ਨਾਲਿ ਖਸਮ ਚਲੈ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ ॥੨੨॥
lines.translit: naanak hukam na chalee naal khasam chalai aradaas |22|
lines.english: O Nanak, no one can issue commands to the Lord Master; let us offer prayers instead. ||22||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਹਲਾ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:
